answer:
This passage appears in Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, Chapter 19 ("Des effets particuliers à chacun des principaux éléments de force dont une loi se compose ; et de l’").[^1]

The chapter uses the example of child exposure (*and infanticide*) in China—where it is *not* criminalized—as a basis for estimating the hypothetical effects if the government *were* to criminalize it (as European governments do). The author calculates that the net benefit of such a law would be vanishingly small amid China's vast population (~353 million): "This good would perhaps not be felt by one single individual out of two hundred thousand, and it would be reduced to almost nothing."[^1]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/19-des-effets-particuliers-a-chacun-des-principaux-el/translation/#p-0cb109).
